extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA attacked from three-man breakaway remnant with under 10km to climb, established 1:05 gap on descent, won by 34 seconds for first non-TT pro victory
tactical_patterns: GANNA demonstrated breakaway specialists can win mountain stages by attacking before descent, using TT power downhill rather than climbing.
THOMAS and RUBIO attacked peloton with 20km left on 24km climb, disrupting chase dynamics when GC teams hesitated.
PERNSTEINER took sprint bonus at finish 34 seconds down, revealing Bora prioritizing stage placings over GC defense.
KELDERMAN survived NIBALI Vincenzo's descent attack, showing Sunweb's GC leader handles technical descents better than expected for pure climber.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: won non-TT stage by recognizing breakaway companions couldn't match his descent power, tactically mature for first GT
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: launched GC-disrupting attack 20km from finish despite not being GC contender, suggests form building mid-race
rider_signals.RUBIO Einer: neutral: followed THOMAS attack intelligently but commentary notes he stuck on wheel without contributing
rider_signals.CARRETERO Héctor: neutral: attacked from three-man break but couldn't sustain effort against GANNA's descent acceleration
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: extended Maglia Rosa lead to 43 seconds over BILBAO, survived NIBALI Vincenzo descent attack without losing GC ground
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: moved to third at 48 seconds, handled technical descent competently against pure descenders
rider_signals.PERNSTEINER Hermann: neutral: finished second at 34 seconds for bonus seconds, Bora using stage tactics rather than pure GC focus
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: descent attack with 7km to go failed to distance GC rivals, suggests lacks explosive acceleration of peak years
